item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to agree with, rumetzen.
Sorry, I just couldn't read through it. This really isn't Secure Contain Protect procedures so much as a written record of this god's exploits. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I've got zero qualms on adding these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This article has a lot of crosslinks as is, but I found a few locations where more could be added.

We told them of how the Enemy would bring plights as horrid as their Red Death but they denied presence of the Enemy.

The Red Death is a reference to SCP-610, so that can be linked.

We told them of how the Enemy would bring plights as horrid as their Red Death but they denied presence of the Enemy.

The following are texts regarding SCP-4017 from a collection of parchment documents initially held by the Praetorian Office of Secret Wisdom1.

The CPSO is originally from SCP-2766, so that can be linked at the first mention.

The following are texts regarding SCP-4017 from a collection of parchment documents initially held by the Praetorian Office of Secret Wisdom2.

Author: Josef, Allied Occult Initiative Special Operative

Josef is a character who appears in another tale by NineVolt, encountering the Norwegian branch of the Church of the Second Hytoth mentioned here. That tale can be linked.

Author: Josef, Allied Occult Initiative Special Operative

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. Cura Praetoria Sapientae Occultae, a division of the Praetorian Guard dedicated to the containment and/or destruction of anomalous creatures and objects that were a threat or inconvenience to the Roman empire.
2. Cura Praetoria Sapientae Occultae, a division of the Praetorian Guard dedicated to the containment and/or destruction of anomalous creatures and objects that were a threat or inconvenience to the Roman empire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did 4017 fade because nobody remembered them/worshipped them, or just because the millennia of fighting wore them down?

A few edits you could make:

place little importance in on gender

Scroll Written by a Followers of Mekhane Member

Why not just "Follower of Mekhane"?

Warrior Sophus descended the interior of Colossus Eight's arm to prevent the Spearman's escape. The Spearman deflected all [melted/burning arrows] which damaged arm gears but missed most spear swings and divine blasts. With great struggle Warrior Sophos impaled the Spearman and made them vanish.

Are these two different warriors or the same warrior spelled differently?

What does "Naevia Victrix" mean? I can't find a translation for it.

When I approached further I was plighted with grisly hallucinations.

I thought "plighted" wasn't a word but apparently it used to be used. However, unless this was originally in English, I don't see why someone translating this in the 1980s would use it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the Roman organisation. Particularly this note

dedicated to the containment and/or destruction of anomalous creatures and objects that were a threat or inconvenience to the Roman empire.

That just strikes me as very Roman as opposed to the Foundation approach: "Huh, an unnatural defiance of reality. Is it actually a problem for us? No? Then we don't care."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm such a sucker for good worldbuilding. This was a pretty easy sell for me. Well, it took me a while to read because length is daunting, but I definitely wanted to read it for a while. Agreeing with Moose, this title is fuckin' great, and the entity itself is unique and cool and I approve. I also love the image of smoke-like gelatinous blood. It gave a real nice feel to the spectral image of the character.

+1, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very good scp from a good goi. Btw, "spectral" is a tag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, snake people, robot dragons, magic arrows, clockwork mechs, aether bombs, wolf cavalry, guardian deities and mobile fortress-trees. Sounds like one hell of a Warhammer game.

Jokes aside, I'm kinda out of the loop when it comes to more minor GOIs but I totally dig this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't quite upvote this, because goddamn there's a lot of wheel-spinning to worldbuild and write pastiches of historical texts. That isn't bad — it's just niche — but it didn't work for me. I did like the use of crosslinks and the careful thought that went into it, though.

I still wanted to comment that I absolutely love the title of the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, it's a lot cleaner than the version I read in draft. Just a sad tale of a hero slowly realising their own irrelevance. Each of the individual tales are interesting (though it's still quite longer overall) and the story has a nice progression to it.
It's a little dense on the cross-links, but beyond that, this is good stuff. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn. You really assembled a who's who of our more history-based GoIs here.

Overall, I was on the fence about this one. On one hand the various documents provide interesting windows into the Foudnationverse across different time periods, which fits the theme of the contest like a glove. On the other hand, most of the content of these entries felt like they didn't establish a whole bunch of new information, and spun there wheels to a degree, making the article drag.

You do enough to build up Onteus-One-Holy's character, however, to make the final interview pack a bit of an emotional punch, enough to push me into the upvote realm.

+1. RIP Onteus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely could’ve written more excerpts, but I didn’t want to add more than what I felt was absolutely necessary for the narrative. Besides, seven collapisbles in a row is already pushing how many collapsibles I can have without making this seem like too daunting of a long read.

Good to hear it all came together well for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I'm not traditionally a fan of one-man-army narratives, and demoniac Nazis still being a major peeve of mine, those are mere nitpicks in comparison to my appreciation of this article as a whole.

I really love how you've woven together so many different historical GoIs and anomalous cultures, mixing entirely fictional ones with anomaly orgs of real ones. I especially like how you wrote it the excerpts from so many different perspectives, showing the various people's different takes on the hero and Ortothan culture in general.

Only thing I am missing is a few more excerpts of descriptions or mentions by the aggressors themselves: an Adymite missive, a Daevan excerpt from SCP-140, an interview or document from the Xia. You've already done really well on the Mekhanite, Roman and Allied descriptions, taking care to write in a tone fitting all groups. If you feel you have the energy left, I'm certain you could write just as immersive accounts from the other perspectives. :)

In any case: +1! Best of luck in the contest!

item.title: Probably my favorite entry so far
item.content:encoded:
Maybe not SINGLE favorite, because I can't choose just one, but this… this is my kind of scp.

Epic mythos building exploring the history of this world, with an amazing character tale amidst it all.

Here's a upvote. HAVE AT THEE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s fair. Other people found the stories interesting enough to enjoy them but I can see why that wouldn’t be the case, which was something I was wondering when I was writing this. Either way I’m not trying something spanning this many eras again any time soon (what with how janky this was too write from jumping between different writing styles as well as the issues you mentioned), heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this a huge slog to get through. Most of the middle section was just contextless descriptions of battle that didn't feel like interesting stories or vignettes that didn't feel like interesting pieces in their own right, and they all just sort of blurred together. They came and went too quickly for me as a reader to really get anything out of them, and overall just added up to a bunch of extra reading time that didn't do much to prepare me for the end. By the time I got to the last two interviews I had basically lost all investment or interest I had in the narrative completely because the text didn't give me anything to attach my interest to besides some vague worldbuilding that was too scattered to form a coherent whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Onteus. Shine on, you crazy diamond.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, this poor guy. D: Probably the best Second Hytoth skip on the site. Not what I would call 4000, but this definitely earned an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The personality of 4000 really grew on me as I read. That last scene felt good, nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor Onteus. Just wanted to be remembered, and then the Foundation had to do what the Foundation does best: be stone-cold bastards.

+1

item.title: And the world goes on
item.content:encoded:
A year back when the 3000 contest was going down (only a year holy shit) I was really interested in participating. However, due to a combination of IRL issues, two failed drafts and attention split between those and a non-3K draft, and motivation flopping, I never got anything posted for it. Now that 4K has come around I was dead set on not letting that repeat, so here's an article for a history theme done in the best way I could think of: what is essentially a love letter to all the site lore and mythos that has been drawing me in since I first joined.

Thanks to Mortos, Cyantreuse, Captain Kirby, Kakroom, stormbreath, ch00bakka, Tufto, and Croquembouche for reading and critting! Thanks to Rimple for coming up with the title.

Image:

  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Korea-Silla-Iron.armor-01.jpg
